questions.question: Why does the one-page constraint work?

options.text: Because shorter documents are easier to email
Because it forces prioritization — everything on the page earned its place
Because most printers only hold one page

questions.explanation: The one-page constraint generates real commitments by making real decisions unavoidable. A 15-page strategy can hold contradictions. One page can't — you're forced to pick one channel, one ICP, one set of daily activities.

questions.question: According to research, how much more likely are written goals to be achieved?
questions.explanation: Written goals are 42% more likely to be achieved than unwritten ones. The one-page system amplifies this through commitment via constraint, daily visibility, and forced accountability.

questions.question: Companies with a one-page strategy document execute how much faster?
questions.explanation: Companies with a one-page strategy document execute 3x faster than those with 20-page documents. Not because one-pagers have less information, but because the constraint forces prioritization.

questions.question: The one-page system is just a simplification exercise — it contains less information than a full strategy.
questions.explanation: False. It's a clarity test, not a simplification exercise. The constraint forces you to eliminate vague aspirations and keep only what's actionable. Everything on the page earned its place through real decisions.

questions.question: Draft your one-page acquisition system. What is your single ICP sentence, your primary channel, your daily activities, and your weekly metrics? Can you fit it on one page?
questions.aiPrompt: Evaluate whether the response creates a concise system with a specific ICP, named primary channel, concrete daily activities, and measurable weekly metrics. Check if it demonstrates the prioritization required by the one-page constraint rather than trying to include everything.
